name: OFM Ontology (Order Fulfilment Management)
description: Models demand-side order fulfilment across two layers: (1) warehouse/distribution — sales orders, stock allocation, storage cost, dispatch, and fulfilment risk; (2) customer/commercial — SLA management, breach detection, landed cost, margin analysis, complaint handling, customer satisfaction, and remediation. 8 LSC-ONT bridges and 9 VE-Series bridges with VP-RRR alignment.

components.entities: SalesOrder (hub, sameAs PurchaseOrder), OrderLine, StockAllocation, StorageEvent, DeliveryWindow, DispatchLeg, CustomerChange, MarginImpact, FulfilmentRisk, DemandPattern, CapacityForecast, ServiceLevelAgreement, OrderMilestone, CustomerNotification, LandedCost, MarginAnalysis, SLABreach, ComplaintCase, CustomerSatisfaction, RemediationAction
components.relationships: 64 relationships including 8 LSC-ONT bridges (F41.2), 9 VE-Series bridges (F41.3), and 15 internal customer/commercial relationships
components.businessRules: 28 rules (20 error, 8 warning severity) in IF-THEN format
components.competencyQuestions: 18 domain-specific CQs with targetEntities, targetRelationships, and targetRules
components.joinPatterns: 16 join patterns including 7 cross-ontology bridge patterns (LSC, VP, PMF, CRT, KPI)

domain: Order Fulfilment / Distribution / Margin Management / Customer Experience
notes: v1.1.0: Customer/commercial layer added (F41.2 + F41.3). 9 new entities, 32 new relationships (15 internal + 8 LSC bridges + 9 VE bridges), 8 new business rules, 7 new join patterns. VP-RRR alignment on 3 VE bridges. SalesOrder marked sameAs PurchaseOrder. Totals: 20 entities, 64 relationships, 28 rules, 16 join patterns, 18 CQs.
